revision.text:
[[ja:八菅神社]] {{infobox religious building |name = Hasuge Shrine|native_name=八菅神社 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image=File:Yasuge shrine.jpg |address=神奈川県愛甲郡愛川町八菅山139[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1079&cd=1207142&scd=&npg=0 神奈川県神社庁] |ISO = JP-14 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 45.14 |経度度 =139|経度分 = 19|経度秒 = 24.38 |祭神=国常立命、伊弉諾尊、伊弉冉尊、誉田別命、金山毘古命、大己貴命、天忍穂耳命 |社格=郷社 |創建= |別名=八菅山大[[権現]]
八菅山七社権現
八菅山 |札所等= |例祭=[[3月28日]]ほか{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.town.aikawa.kanagawa.jp/aikawa_kankoukyoukai/event/1512707728046.html|title=イベント|publisher=愛川町観光協会|accessdate=2021-11-28}} |神事= |地図 = Japan Kanagawa |prefecture={{ill|Aikawa|qid=Q1203267|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.5125|N|139.323389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Hasuge Shrine''' (八菅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on {{ill|Hasugeyama|ja|八菅山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugeyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|Aikawa, Kanagawa|en|Aikawa, Kanagawa|qid=Q1203267|lt=Aikawa, Kanagawa|1=Aikawa, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Aikō District, Kanagawa|en|Aikō District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202818|lt=Aikō District|1=Aikō District, Kanagawa}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It held the status of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == History == Hasuge Shrine was historically one of the most significant centers of {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|ja|関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō region|1=Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655841}}. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|qid=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} (1868), with the issuance of the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏判然令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}}, the process of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} took place. This led to the destruction of Buddhist elements in what was once a syncretic practice due to the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} movement. Consequently, Hasuge Shrine was established as a separate Shinto shrine under the name Gōsha Hasuge Shrine. Prior to this, Hasuge Shrine was known as '''Hasugeyama Kōshō-ji''' (八菅山光勝寺), and several large stone monuments from that era have been preserved: - A monument from {{ill|Shōō (Kamakura period)|en|Shōō (Kamakura period)|qid=Q1327371|lt=Shōō|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} 4 (1291), erected by a Shugendō practitioner named Chōki, who led a training pilgrimage alongside Matsuda Sōjō and Kumano Chōshōshū Kenkyū. It stands about 3.8 meters tall. - A monument from {{ill|Tenmon (era)|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon (era)|1=Tenmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21096812}} 21 (1552), placed at the time of a visit by {{ill|Dōzō|ja|道増|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōzō|1=Dōzō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132435689}}, a head priest of {{ill|Shōgo-in|en|Shōgo-in|qid=Q8664338|lt=Shōgo-in Temple|1=Shōgo-in}}, measuring approximately 2.3 meters. - A monument from {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 12 (1841), over 6 meters tall, placed during the visit of Prince Yūjin (雄仁親王), another head priest of Shōgo-in. According to records such as the *Hasugeyama Chishi Chōshoage* (『八菅山地誌調書上』, Bunsei 9 [1826]) and the *Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki Kō* (『新編相模国風土記稿』, compiled in Tenpō 12 [1841]), Hasugeyama Kōshō-ji became a branch temple of Shōgo-in during the {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku era|1=Genroku}} period or possibly as early as {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|qid=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō era|1=Jōkyō}} 4 (1687). During the medieval period, the temple enshrined the '''Seven Shrines of Gongen''' (七所権現), which later became known as the '''Seven Shrines of Hasugeyama''' (七社権現) and '''Great Gongen of Hasugeyama''' (八菅山大権現). The seven shrines included: 1. {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} 2. {{ill|Zaō Gongen|ja|蔵王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaō Gongen|qq=}} 3. {{ill|Hakone Gongen|ja|箱根権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakone Gongen|qq=}} 4. {{ill|Otoko-yama Hachiman|ja|男山八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko-yama Hachiman|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 5. {{ill|Sannō Gongen|ja|山王権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Gongen|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 6. {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}} 7. {{ill|Izu Hashiriyu Gongen|ja|伊豆走湯権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Hashiriyu Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Hasugeyama Kōshō-ji was a large temple complex, consisting of the Seven Shrines of Gongen, a worship hall, the Kokumine Kanjō-dō (国峰灌頂堂, a meditation hall), a main hall, a hall dedicated to {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|qid=Q198946|lt=En no Gyōja|1=En no Gyōja}}, an {{ill|Tōshō-gū|en|Tōshō-gū|qid=Q1190456|lt=Tōshō-gū|1=Tōshō-gū}}, a bell tower, and numerous lodgings for monks. According to the *Hasugeyama Chishi Chōshoage* and the research of Japanese scholar Takao Shirokawa, by the end of the Edo period, there were 34 monk residences (坊) on the mountain. Hasugeyama was the largest branch of Shōgo-in in South Kanto and had deep ties to the head temple in Kyoto. == Shugendō Training at Hasugeyama == Hasugeyama was a key site for *Mineiri* (峯入り), or mountain ascetic training, in which practitioners followed a pilgrimage route through the mountains of Hasuge and {{ill|Mount Ōyama (Kanagawa)|en|Mount Ōyama (Kanagawa)|qid=Q386518|lt=Ōyama (Kanagawa)|1=Mount Ōyama (Kanagawa)}}. The *Spring Pilgrimage* was conducted from February 20 to April 8, while the *Autumn Pilgrimage* ran from July 20 to September 8. Initially lasting 49 days, the training was shortened to 35 days in {{ill|Kōji (era)|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji (era)|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), and the autumn pilgrimage was discontinued in {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|qid=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 3 (1560). During the Edo period, the *Mineiri* at Hasugeyama was recognized by Shōgo-in as a "National Peak" (国峰), meaning that completing nine training sessions at Hasugeyama was equivalent to completing one pilgrimage at {{ill|Mount Ōmine|en|Mount Ōmine|qid=Q3695989|lt=Ōmine|1=Mount Ōmine}} in terms of Rank advancement. == Meiji Period and the End of Hasuge Shugendō == With the {{ill|Abolition of Shugendō|ja|修験宗廃止令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abolition of Shugendō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Meiji 5 (1872), the Shugendō settlement at Hasugeyama was dissolved. The mountain's resident monks secularized and settled in the surrounding areas, often taking new surnames such as {{ill|Adachihara|ja|足立原|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachihara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or Chiba (千葉). Some families retained pride in their mountain heritage and identified themselves as descendants of "Oni" (鬼, demon-like figures in Japanese folklore). Today, Hasuge Shrine continues the traditions of Shugendō in its *Spring Grand Festival*, which features a {{ill|Firewalking|en|Firewalking|qid=Q929202|lt=fire-walking|1=Firewalking}} ritual and a {{ill|saitō goma|ja|採燈護摩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saitō goma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ceremony. == Sacred Precincts and Surroundings == * Hasuge Shrine’s forest is a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|qid=Q23790|lt=Natural monument|1=Natural monument}} designated by Kanagawa Prefecture.[http://www.planet.pref.kanagawa.jp/app/search/cul/info?REG_ID=CULB01A-0002760&andor=and&page=1&word=&key2=%40412%40&CATEGORY=cul&range=50 Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Heritage] * The shrine houses a treasure hall.[http://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/museum/jinja/14/14_hasuge.html Kokugakuin University Shrine Museum Encyclopedia] == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Hasugeyama Ikoi no Mori|ja|八菅山いこいの森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugeyama Ikoi no Mori|1=hasugesan'ikoinomori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21654175}} * {{ill|Oyama Kōchi|ja|尾山耕地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Kōchi|1=oyamakouchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20043571}} == Access == * From {{ill|Hon-Atsugi Station|en|Hon-Atsugi Station|qid=Q4499730|lt=Hon-Atsugi Station|1=Hon-Atsugi Station}} ({{ill|Odakyu Odawara Line|ja|小田急電鉄小田原線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Odakyu Odawara Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), take a {{ill|Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|ja|神奈川中央交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|1=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10854960}} bus (routes 厚59, 厚60, 厚64, 厚66, 厚96). * From {{ill|Ebina Station|en|Ebina Station|qid=Q865932|lt=Ebina Station|1=Ebina Station}}, take a {{ill|Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|ja|神奈川中央交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|1=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10854960}} bus (route 海09). * Get off at the Ippommatsu bus stop and walk 20 minutes. * Alternatively, take the {{ill|Aikawa Town Circulation Bus|ja|愛川町内循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aikawa Town Circulation Bus|1=Aikawa Town Loop Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17190630}} and get off at the Ikoi no Mori Entrance stop, then walk 6 minutes. == References == == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588941}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=1079&cd=1207142&scd=&npg=0 Hasuge Shrine] - Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association {{shinto-stub}}{{神道 横}}]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]]]]]]]] Town]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aikawa]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu_shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]]
